data.title: Megawide Construction Fined Over Cash Flow Misstatement
data.description:
Megawide Construction said the Securities and Exchange Commission of Philippines fined the company 76,111 Philippine pesos for a material misstatement in its cash flow statement.

The misstatement in the statement filed on Nov. 14, 2024, was because of differing views between the external auditor and management on classifying certain cash flow items, according to a Monday Philippine Exchange filing.

However, the net cash movement and ending balance remained unchanged before and after the auditor's review.

The construction company is required to pay the penalty by March 3, it added.

data.title: Higher Debt Issuance in Eurozone Could Slow Decline in Bond Yields — Market Talk
data.description: Higher bond supply to fund defence spending presents bearish risk for eurozone duration, says Citi Research's Jamie Searle in a note. While higher supply could slow a decline in bond yields, it is unlikely to prevent it, he says. Therefore, Citi Research continues to expect 10-year Bund yields to track back to the lower half of the 2%-2.5% range in the coming weeks or months. However, there is an emerging risk that this return to lower yield levels might take longer than thought, given issuance pressure and the possible compromise of a slowing to quarterly rate cuts by the European Central Bank before trough rate is reached, he says. The 10-year Bund yield closed at 2.47% on Friday, according to Tradeweb.(emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: South Korea's Kospi Ends 0.35% Lower at 2645.27
Exosens Books Order for Photonis 4G Intensifier Tubes from Senop
data.description:
Exosens received an order for Photonis white phosphor 4G intensifier tubes from Finnish provider of high-tech optronic solutions Senop.

The French electro-optical technology company said the order marks its third contract with Senop since 2021, according to a Monday release. Delivery is expected throughout 2025.

data.title: Energy Infrastructure Partners to invest 150 million euros to up BayWa r.e stake
data.description:
Swiss energy investor Energy Infrastructure Partners (EIP) said on Monday it will invest 150 million euros ($157.61 million) to raise its stake from 49% to 65% in Germany's BayWa r.e.

German agricultural group BayWa said earlier that EIP would acquire a majority stake in its renewable energy unit as part of a capital increase.

($1 = 0.9517 euros)

data.title: Lifestyle Communities Posts Lower H1 Earnings Despite Higher Revenue
data.description:
Lifestyle Communities reported Monday that its attributable earnings per diluted share in the first half fell to AU$0.1857, from AU$0.1967 per diluted share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha were expecting an EPS of AU$0.174.

Revenue from ordinary activities for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$115.9 million, up 16% from the same period last year.

Home settlement revenue rose to AU$84.9 million from AU$70.3 million year on year.

Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected AU$102.1 million in revenue.

LIC also said that it appointed Henry Ruiz, former Chief Executive Officer of REA Group , as CEO effective March 5, per the filing.

The board did not declare an interim dividend for the reported period.

data.title: Amgen to invest $200 million in India site, CEO says
data.description:
U.S. drugmaker Amgen will invest about $200 million this year in its new technology centre in southern India, with further investments planned, Chief Executive Officer Robert Bradway said at the inauguration of the site on Monday.

Amgen announced its plans to open a "technology and innovation" site in India last year that is focused on increasing the use of artificial intelligence and data science to support development of new medicines.

The drugmaker is one of many pharmaceutical giants looking to expand their presence in India, the so-called "pharmacy of the world", and has emerged as a hub for global capability centres in recent years.

"We have wanted for a long time to expand Amgen's presence in India and that time has come. Amgen is investing an initial $200 million in this centre in 2025 and plan to make additional significant investments in future years," Bradway said.

The site in Hyderabad, capital of the state of Telangana, is expected to have a workforce of about 2,000 by the end of the year, with around 300 employees already working there, said Som Chattopadhyay, national executive for India at Amgen.

The city will also host the BioAsia conference this week, where executives from global drugmakers including Amgen, Eli Lilly and Novartis , as well as several Indian pharmaceutical giants will be speaking.

data.title: European stock futures, euro climb as Germany stays mainstream
data.description:
Key points:
  • DAX up, Euro hits 1-month high as Merz wins German vote
  • Wall St futures bounce ahead of Nvidia earnings
  • Asia stocks dip, trade thin with Tokyo on holiday

European shares and the euro climbed on Monday as Germany's election produced a mainstream outcome, while Wall Street futures firmed on hopes results from AI diva Nvidia this week would justify the tech sector's sky-high valuations.

DAX futures jumped 1.2%, while the single currency rose 0.44% to $1.0507 and looked set to test its January top at $1.0535. EUROSTOXX 50 futures added 0.66% and FTSE futures 0.1%.

German's new conservative leader Friedrich Merz still has to form a coalition government and it is not yet clear whether that will include one or two partners, with the latter likely to take more time and horse trading.

The uncertainty comes as European Union leaders are set to hold an extraordinary summit on March 6 to discuss additional support for Ukraine and how to pay for European defence needs.

Liquidity was thinned by a holiday in Tokyo markets and MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) dipped 0.5%. Nikkei futures (NKc1) traded at 38,310, under the cash close of 38,776.

Chinese blue chips eased 0.26%. Hong Kong shares retreated 0.34%, giving up some gains from their recent tech-driven bull run.

"Chinese authorities have made a meaningful pivot in their rhetoric towards Big Tech and the private economy at large," said Barclays analysts in a note.

"It may imply a policy shift intended to unwind very low local equity valuations, encourage inflows into China, and could moderate our bullish broad dollar and cautious EM views."

S&P 500 futures and Nasdaq futures both gained 0.5%. The Nasdaq had fallen 2.5% last week, its worst week in three months, with losses led by the Magnificent Seven.

That pullback raised the stakes for Nvidia's results on Wednesday where investors are looking for fourth-quarter sales around $38.5 billion and first-quarter guidance around $42.5 billion.

As usual, options point to a share price move of around 8% in either direction should the results surprise.

Wall Street had taken a hit on Friday when a survey on services showed a shock slide in activity amid concerns about tariffs and cost pressures. There were even reports the White House was pressuring Mexico to put its own tariffs on Chinese imports as part of a deal.

INFLATION SCARE

The Federal Reserve's favoured measure of core inflation is due on Friday and expected to show a slowdown to 2.6% from 2.8%, but could be overshadowed by tariff worries.

A survey of U.S. consumers out on Friday showed inflation expectations for 5 to 10 years ahead climbed to 3.5%, the highest since 1995.

"Longer-run inflation expectations are at risk of becoming de-anchored," warned analysts at ANZ in a note. "That was the clear message from the subset of soft U.S. economic surveys released on Friday, and for the Fed, the data signal that enhanced caution is required."

At least nine Fed officials are speaking this week, some of them more than once, and are likely to reiterate the cautious message on further rate cuts.

Markets are not priced for an easing until July, with just two cuts implied all year. (0#USDIRPR)

Treasuries rallied in the wake of the soft services data, though inflation and supply uncertainties remain a hurdle and 10-year futures (TYc1) were down 5 ticks on Monday.

The drop in Treasury yields, particularly in real terms, has weighed on the dollar against the yen as Japanese yields rise on speculation of another rate hike from the Bank of Japan.

The dollar was pinned at 149.46 yen , having shed 2% last week to threaten chart support at 148.65. The dollar index dipped 0.2% to 106.31, with losses on the euro, sterling and Swiss franc.

In commodity markets, gold remained well supported at $2,937 an ounce , having climbed for eight weeks in a row.

Oil has been heading in the other direction in part on speculation an eventual peace deal on Ukraine could see sanctions eased on Russia, boosting its oil exports.

Brent slipped 6 cents to $74.37 a barrel, while U.S. crude fell 13 cents to $70.27 per barrel having hit a two-month low earlier.

data.title: Greentown China to Pay $452 Million in Tender Offer of Outstanding Bonds
data.description:
Greentown China Holdings recorded successful tenders for outstanding bonds worth $451.9 million on Monday and plans to accept all the tendered bonds, a same-day bourse filing said.

On Feb. 13, the Chinese property developer offered to buy back outstanding bonds totaling $741 million. This included $446.5 million of its 4.7% senior bonds and $294.5 million of its 5.65% senior bonds, which are due in April and July, respectively.

The accepted bonds are expected to be paid by Tuesday. Following the payment, the 2025 April bonds and 2025 July bonds will be left with outstanding principal amounts of $134.3 million and $154.8 million respectively.

data.title: U.S. 10-Year Treasury Yield Expected to Fall in Coming Months — Market Talk
data.description:
0643 GMT - The 10-year U.S. Treasury yield could move lower in the coming months, alongside anticipated rate cuts by the Federal Reserve, says Mediolanum International Funds Ltd's Marco Meijer. "​In the near term, we expect 10-year U.S. Treasury yields to fall toward 4% by the summer, as uncertainty around tariffs has dampened the U.S. growth outlook," the deputy head of fixed income says. At the same time, some progress in reducing the budget deficit could further support a downward move in yields, he says. Mediolanum expects the Fed to deliver three rate cuts of 25 basis points each this year but acknowledges that the risks are skewed towards fewer rate cuts. The 10-year Treasury yield is up 1 basis point to 4.430%, according to LSEG. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0612 GMT - China will likely to set 2025's economic growth target around 5%, BofA Securities analysts write in a note. This year's GDP growth target will deliver few surprises, despite the risks from tariffs, they add. The national GDP growth target is usually in line with provincial targets released earlier. The three largest provinces in China--Guangdong, Shandong and Jiangsu--have all maintained their growth target at the same level as last year's, suggesting the national GDP target should stay the same, they say. Beijing will likely hint at further lowering of interest rates and banks' reserve requirement ratio, they add. The policymakers may set a more aggressive budget deficit of at least 3.8% to shore up fiscal stimulus efforts, they say. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0529 GMT - The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield's downside risk is increasing, says Quek Ser Leang of UOB Global Economics & Markets Research in a research report. The yield decisively broke below the bottom of the daily Ichimoku cloud last Friday, the markets strategist notes. This break, together with the weekly moving average convergence divergence indicator turning negative, suggests rising downside risk in the 10-year yield. The level to watch is 4.335%, the top of the weekly Ichimoku cloud, followed by the 55-week exponential moving average, which is now at 4.265%, he adds. The U.S. 10-year Treasury yield is little changed at 4.4196%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0422 GMT - Malaysia's consumer inflation could rise to 2.6% this year from 1.8% in 2024, due to the gradual withdrawal of the RON95 fuel subsidy from mid-year, Apex Securities analyst Afham Zulghafir says in a note. Sales tax expansion and likely rise in consumer demand due to rising household incomes could also push prices higher, he says. The country's GDP growth could remain strong at 4.6% in 2025, compared with 5.1% last year, supported by resilient domestic demand and investment spending, he reckons. With CPI remaining within the official target range of 2.0%-3.5%, the analyst expects Bank Negara to maintain the policy rate at 3.0% in 2025, supporting Malaysia's economic growth.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0411 GMT - China's official manufacturing PMI likely indicated another contraction in February with a 49.8 reading, Moody's Analytics says in a note. "Even as production chugs along to fulfill existing orders, a further 10% tariff in the U.S. on goods from China should hit new orders," it says. China's official gauge of factory activity contracted to 49.1 in January from December's 50.1 as factories suspended operations days ahead of the Lunar New Year holiday. As for the non-manufacturing PMI, retail sales should get a boost from Lunar New Year activity but construction will likely stay in the doldrums, it says. China's manufacturing PMI data is due Saturday. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

0338 GMT - The Singapore dollar strengthens against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian session, driven by risk-on sentiment fueled by gains in U.S. stock-index futures and projected results from Germany's election. Initial estimates showed the CDU and its CSU sister party securing just under 29% of the vote, followed by Alternative for Germany, or AfD, with around 20%. Given that the election outcome aligns with expectations, analysts at Maybank say there are hopes of reduced political uncertainty. Additionally, signs of fading U.S. exceptionalism have emerged, supported by recent economic data. USD/SGD is 0.3% lower at 1.3319. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0222 GMT - More upside for JGB yields and the yen looks likely, Capital Economics' John Higgins says in a commentary. Japanese inflation and PMI data released Friday bolster Capital Economics' view that the JGB 10-year yield will continue to rise, the chief markets economist says. The ongoing rise in JGB yields probably stems from investors' reassessment of the Bank of Japan's monetary-policy outlook, Higgins says. The BOJ's policy rate is likely to reach 1.25% by end-2026, which feeds into Capital Economics' forecast for JGB 10-year yield to end 2025 at 1.75%. This could also buoy the yen against the dollar, says Capital Economics, which forecasts USD/JPY to reach 145 by end-2025. USD/JPY is 0.1% higher at 149.46. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0219 GMT - Singapore's CPI likely rose 1.95% on year in January, according to the median estimate of six economists polled by The Wall Street Journal. That would compare with December's 1.6% increase. Headline inflation is expected to pick up due to a higher contribution from accommodation costs and private road transport, Sanjay Mathur, chief economist for Southeast Asia and India at ANZ, says in a note. Core inflation is projected to have eased to 1.6% last month from December's 1.8% rise, according to five of the economists polled. Core inflation will likely continue declining, partly due to last year's higher base from the GST hike, ANZ says. The data are due Monday. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0123 GMT - Asian currencies strengthen against the U.S. dollar in early trade amid prospects of Fed rate cuts that would decrease the allure of U.S. fixed-income assets and demand for the greenback. U.S. economic data released last Friday were disappointing, NAB's Rodrigo Catril says in commentary. Also, "playing to the emerging theme of a loss of U.S. exceptionalism, other services PMIs around the globe showed improvements," the senior FX strategist says. The weak U.S. data led to overnight-index-swap market bringing forward a 25 bp rate cut by the Fed to July from September, Catril adds. USD/KRW falls 0.2% to 1,431.80; AUD/USD edges 0.1% higher to 0.6369. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0035 GMT - Euro rises against other currencies in the early Asian session as traders digest the outcome of Germany's election. Exit polls and early vote-counting suggest the Christian Democratic Union won around 29% of votes and will probably lead a coalition government, CBA's Global Economic & Markets Research team say in a research report. "However, it is unclear whether two or three parties will be needed to form a coalition government," the team says. Market participants will likely be closely watching whether the eventual government agrees to reform the 'debt brake' to support economic growth, the team adds. EUR/USD rises 0.3% to 1.0499; EUR/JPY gains 0.2% to 156.42. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0025 GMT - South Korea's benchmark Kospi falls 0.6% to 2638.50 in early trade as semiconductor and steel stocks retreat. Foreign and institutional investors are net sellers, as Wall Street's Friday falls are weighing on sentiment. Memory-chip makers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix decline 1.4% and 3.3%, respectively. Chip-making equipment supplier Hanmi Semiconductor loses 4.6%. Steelmaker Dongguk Steel slides 3.1%, paring Friday's sharp gains fueled by Seoul's move to impose anti-dumping duties on Chinese steel products. USD/KRW is 0.2% higher at 1,436.50 in Seoul onshore trading. South Korea's 10-year government bond yield is down 3.2 bps at 2.856%. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Great Wall Motor's Unit Acquires 80% Stake in Electrical Component Firm
data.description:
=-0Great Wall Motor's unit Nobo Technology agreed to acquire 80% equity interest in Wuxi Xindong Semiconductor Technology from Wensheng Technology for nearly 3.8 million yuan, a Feb. 21 filing with the Hong Kong bourse stated.

Following completion, Nobo Technology will hold 80% equity interest in Wuxi Xindong, with automaker Great Wall Motor will own 20%.

Wuxi Xindong Semiconductor Technology engages mainly in the manufacture and sale of electrical components among other business activities.

The stake purchase will allow the firm to vertically integrate its industries and boost production and operational efficiency.

Shares of the form were up over 2% in recent trade.

Leaf Global Wins Tree Plantation Project in Saudi Arabia

data.title: Italy's Saipem to Combine Energy Services With Norwegian Rival Subsea7 in Merger of Equals
data.description:
Italian oilfield services company Saipem (SPM.MI) will merge with Norwegian peer Subsea7 to create a global market leader in the subsea and engineering and construction offshore sector, according to a late Sunday release.

A signed memorandum of understanding for the principal terms of the proposed merger entails the transfer of 6.688 Saipem shares for each Subsea7 share held. Concurrently, Reuters, in a same-day report, pegged the value of the planned transaction at $4.63 billion.

The memorandum also calls for the distribution of an extraordinary dividend payment of 450 million euros to Subsea7 shareholders before the completion of the European Union cross-border statutory merger, which will be carried out by way of incorporation of Subsea 7 into Saipem. The shareholders of both entities will own an equal 50% stake in the combined company, which will be named Saipem7 and listed on both the Milan and Oslo bourses.

At a time when the size of clients' projects is growing, the new Milan-based entity will offer a diversified fleet of over 60 construction vessels with offshore and onshore services that range from drilling, engineering, and construction to life-of-field services and decommissioning. Saipem7's revenue and backlog will total 20 billion euros and 43 billion euros, respectively.

Owing to the fleet optimization, procurement, sales and marketing, and process efficiencies, the companies expect annual synergies of 300 million euros in the third year after completion, which is expected in the second half of 2026. The one-off costs to generate such synergies are estimated at 270 million euros.

Saipem's controlling shareholders Eni (ENI.MI) and Italian sovereign wealth fund Cassa Depositi e Prestiti Equity also signed a memorandum of understanding with Subsea7's largest shareholder, Siem Industries, regarding their support of the proposed deal, according to a separate release on Sunday. The three companies will appoint the majority of the combined entity's board and serve as its core key shareholders with a combined 29% stake.

"Saipem and Subsea7 exhibit strong geographical and customer-base complementarity," the Italian integrated energy company Eni said. "Their combination will enhance the offering of products and services while optimizing fleet utilization. As a result, the merged company will be better positioned to seize strategic and commercial opportunities arising from the evolution of the oil&gas market and the changes linked to the Energy transition."

data.title: Moreld Completes Debt Refinancing
data.description:
Moreld said Monday it completed the refinancing of its debt.

The industrial multi-disciplinary engineering group said the transaction included the full redemption of the outstanding portion of the $225 million senior secured notes due 2029 issued by its Aurora Group subsidiary.

Moreld said it used net proceeds from its issue in January of a $130 million senior secured bond with a five-year term to help refinance its debt.

data.title: Nordic Halibut AS - Announces final investment decision for the Tingvoll production facility
data.description:
Averøy 24.02.2025

Nordic Halibut AS ("NOHAL" or the "Company") has reached the final investmentdecision for the Tingvoll production facility, marking a significant milestonein the company's growth strategy. Mobilization will commence shortly, withplanned construction start during Q1 2025. The facility is planned to be fullyoperational by 2027.

The new Tingvoll production facility is set to significantly enhanceefficiencyand production capacity as part of the company's ramp-up plan, targeting anannual production of 10,350 tonnes HOG by 2031.

-We are now establishing a solid foundation for sustainable profitable growth.As Phase 2 reaches full operational capacity, we will have a highly efficient,cash-flow-generating platform that not only strengthens our financial positionbut also provides significant investment capacity and drive long-termexpansion", CEO Edvard Henden.

Further updates on project progress and key milestones will be provided in duecourse.

About the Tingvoll facility:

NOHAL is developing and building a new land-based facility with all productionphases integrated at one location, targeting an annual production of 1 millionjuveniles released into the sea. Coupled with the company's already existingland-based infrastructure, it will have the capacity to put over 2 millionfishto sea per year, corresponding to a harvest volume of 10,350 tonnes HOG.

The facility will be built with well-established and proven technology andwillbe based on a 100% flow-through system. The fjords surrounding Torjulvågen,Tingvoll, are deep, enabling NOHAL to access water of high quality and stabletemperature, perfectly suited for land-based halibut production without theneedfor advanced technological water treatment or heating. This makes the facilityenergy-efficient, in addition to minimizing technological risks throughout theproduction cycle.

As part of the company's sustainability ambition, NOHAL has designed the newland-based facility to be nearly self-sufficient by utilizing its ownrenewablesolar power installed on the rooftops of the production units. Additionally,thefacility will serve as a center of competence and R&D development, furtherstrengthening NOHAL's competitive advantage.

- The new land-based facility will, for the first time in the company'shistory,be specially designed for halibut farming, without any legacy costs in termsoflocation and technology. Utilizing Torjulvågen's natural environment and waterproperties enables us to produce energy-efficient and sustainable superfood atan industrial scale, says CEO Edvard Henden.

For more information, please contact:

CEO Edvard Hendenedvard@nordichalibut.no+47 911 41 165

or

CFO Are Strandare.strand@nordichalibut.no+47 91539232

About Nordic Halibut AS:Nordic Halibut is a leading farmer of Atlantic halibut located in WesternNorwayand with headquarters in Averøy, Nordmøre. The Company has a fully integratedand well-developed value chain from genetics to sales and has had asignificantbreakthrough in early phase production. A growth plan to expand productionvolumes is implemented with production target of 4,500 tonnes HOG within 2027and 10,350 tonnes HOG within 2031. Nordic Halibut will create shareholdervalueby pursuing value accretive organic growth through increased production.

https://newsweb.oslobors.no/message/639498

data.title: Prosus to Buy Just Eat Takeaway for $4.29 Billion to Create EU Food Delivery Giant
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Amsterdam-listed investment group Prosus agreed to buy Just Eat Takeaway.com for 4.1 billion euros ($4.29 billion) in cash to create the fourth largest food delivery group globally.

Prosus said it reached a conditional agreement to acquire the food-delivery group for 20.30 euros a share in cash. This represents a 49% premium to the shares' three-month volume weighted average price and a 22% premium to its three-month high.

Prosus said it sees an opportunity to accelerate growth at Just Eat through the deal, supported by its strong industry experience to innovate and drive efficiencies.

"We are excited for Just Eat Takeaway.com to join the Prosus Group and the opportunity to create a European tech champion. Prosus already has an extensive food delivery portfolio outside of Europe and a proven track record of profitable growth through investment in our customer and driver experiences, restaurant partnerships, and world-class logistics, powered by innovation and AI," Prosus Chief Executive Officer Fabricio Bloisi said.

The offer was unanimously supported by Just Eat's management board and supervisory Board. The deal is subject to regulatory approvals.

"Just Eat Takeaway.com is now a faster growing, more profitable and predominantly European-based business. Prosus fully supports our strategic plans, and its extensive resources will help to further accelerate our investments and growth across food, groceries, fintech and other adjacencies," Just Eat Chief executive Officer Jitse Groen said.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Koh Brothers to Dispose Off Freehold Land in Malaysia
data.description:
Koh Brothers Group's indirect wholly owned subsidiary, G & W Industries (M), entered into a sale and purchase agreement with an unrelated third party to dispose of a piece of freehold land at Negeri Johor, Malaysia.

The total consideration for the disposal is 81.5 million Malaysian ringgit, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Monday.

The land has a total area of 5.4034 hectares and is currently vacant.

The company plans to use net proceeds worth SG$20.1 million for general working capital of the group.

data.title: S Chand Forms New Step-Down Subsidiary for Printing, Publishing Business
data.description:
Publishing company S Chand and Co. has incorporated a step-down subsidiary named Shri Shyamlal Printing Press, according to a Saturday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The new entity is 100% owned by Vikas Publishing House, a wholly-owned subsidiary of the company.

Shri Shyamlal Printing Press will be in the business of printing and publishing.

Shares of the company were down nearly 3% in recent trade.

data.description:

To access the full report and subscribe to future updates, visit https://www.carnegie.se/en/commissioned-research/cag-group/
